<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/michael.abebe/Code/materialize && git diff --cached</summary>

```
[33mdiff --git a/.claude/skills/mz-pr-review/SKILL.md b/.claude/skills/mz-pr-review/SKILL.md[m
[33mnew file mode 100644[m
[33mindex 0000000000..c32d620802[m
[33m--- /dev/null[m
[33m+++ b/.claude/skills/mz-pr-review/SKILL.md[m
[35m@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@[m
[32m+[m[32m---[m
[32m+[m[32mdescription:  &gt;[m
[32m+[m[32mLocal PR review against Materialize standards.[m
[32m+[m[32mThis skill should be used when the user asks for a review of changes.[m
[32m+[m[32margument-hint: [base-branch][m
[32m+[m[32mallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Grep, Glob, Task][m
[32m+[m[32m---[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mPerform a local code review of the current branch's changes against Materialize project standards.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Steps[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m1. Parse arguments from: $ARGUMENTS — optional base branch to diff against (default: &#96;main&#96;).[m
[32m+[m[32m2. Get the diff: &#96;git diff &lt;base&gt;...HEAD&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m3. Get the file list: &#96;git diff --stat &lt;base&gt;...HEAD&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m4. Review the diff against the checklists below.[m
[32m+[m[32m5. Present findings organized as: **Blocking**, **Strong suggestions**, **Nits**.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Review checklist[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mThe overall developer guide for reviewing changes is defined in &#96;doc/developer/guide-changes.md&#96;, always read and follow its guidance.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Tests[m
[32m+[m[32m- Every behavior change has at least one new or modified test.[m
[32m+[m[32m- SQL/query behavior → look for &#96;.slt&#96; in &#96;test/sqllogictest/&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Wire/protocol behavior → look for &#96;.pt&#96; in &#96;test/pgtest/&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Rust logic/types/APIs → add or extend a Rust unit test in the crate (e.g. &#96;#[cfg(test)]&#96; or &#96;tests/&#96;); run with &#96;cargo test -p mz-&lt;crate&gt;&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Prefer testing observable behavior (SQL results, wire protocol) over implementation details.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Red flag: behavior change with no test changes.[m
[32m+[m[32m- For more testing guidelines, read &#96;doc/developer/guide-testing.md&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Code style (Rust)[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Imports:** &#96;std&#96; → external crates → &#96;crate::&#96;; one &#96;use&#96; per module; prefer &#96;crate::&#96; over &#96;super::&#96; in non-test code.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Errors:** Structured with &#96;thiserror&#96;; no bare &#96;anyhow!("...")&#96;. &#96;Display&#96; should not print full error chain.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Async:** Use &#96;ore::task::spawn&#96; / &#96;spawn_blocking&#96;, not raw &#96;tokio::spawn&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Tests:** &#96;#[mz_ore::test]&#96;; panic in tests rather than returning &#96;Result&#96;.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Code style (SQL)[m
[32m+[m[32m- Keywords capitalized (&#96;SELECT&#96;, &#96;FROM&#96;); identifiers lowercase.[m
[32m+[m[32m- No space between function name and &#96;(&#96;.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Error messages[m
[32m+[m[32m- Primary: short, factual, lowercase first letter, no trailing punctuation.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Detail/hint: complete sentences, capitalized, period.[m
[32m+[m[32m- No "unable", "bad", "illegal", "unknown"; say what kind of object.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Architecture[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Simplicity:** No incidental complexity; simplify redundant logic.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **No special casing:** Prefer composable design over extra booleans/branches.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Encapsulation:** sql-parser = grammar only (no semantic validation); sql = planning + semantics.[m
[32m+[m[32m- **Dependencies:** New crates must be justified.[m
[32m+[m[32m- For more design guidelines read: &#96;doc/developer/best-practices.md&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Polish[m
[32m+[m[32m- No leftover &#96;// XXX&#96;, &#96;// FIXME&#96;, &#96;dbg!&#96;, &#96;println!&#96;, or commented-out code.[m
[32m+[m[32m- No unrelated formatting changes in untouched code.[m
[32m+[m[32m- New public items should have doc comments.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Release notes[m
[32m+[m[32m- Any user-visible change to stable APIs needs a release note (imperative, "This release will…").[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## One semantic change rule[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mThe PR should do one thing. If it spans multiple CODEOWNERS areas (e.g. sql-parser + sql planner), consider suggesting a split.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Rules[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m- Review the code, not the author. Explain the *why* behind suggestions.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Use **nit:** for preferences where reasonable people could disagree.[m
[32m+[m[32m- If the PR improves overall codebase health and blocking items are addressed, say so.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Do NOT make any changes — this is read-only review.[m
\ No newline at end of file[m

```
</details>
</tool-use>

---


Staged change is adding the new file `.claude/skills/mz-pr-review/SKILL.md` (the PR review skill). Branch has no issue number. Suggested commit message:

---

```
[Tooling] Add mz-pr-review skill for local PR review

Add a Claude skill that runs a local code review against Materialize
standards: diff against a base branch, then check the change against
tests (including Rust unit tests), code style, errors, architecture,
and polish. Findings are reported as Blocking / Strong suggestions /
Nits. The checklist aligns with doc/developer/guide-changes.md and
guide-testing.md.
```

---

Confirm if you want this used as the commit message or if you’d like any edits (e.g. different area, more/less detail). I won’t commit or push until you confirm.

```
[33mdiff --git a/.claude/skills/mz-tests/SKILL.md b/.claude/skills/mz-tests/SKILL.md[m
[33mnew file mode 100644[m
[33mindex 0000000000..439a073917[m
[33m--- /dev/null[m
[33m+++ b/.claude/skills/mz-tests/SKILL.md[m
[35m@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@[m
[32m+[m[32m---[m
[32m+[m[32mdescription: Run or add Materialize tests (slt, testdrive, pgtest, cargo test)[m
[32m+[m[32margument-hint: &lt;test-type&gt; &lt;file-or-pattern&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32mallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Glob, Task][m
[32m+[m[32m---[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mRun or help add tests in the Materialize repo. Supports sqllogictest, testdrive, pgtest, and Rust unit tests.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Test framework quick reference[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m| What you're testing | Where | Format | How to run |[m
[32m+[m[32m|---|---|---|---|[m
[32m+[m[32m| SQL correctness, types, functions | &#96;test/sqllogictest/&#96; | &#96;.slt&#96; | &#96;bin/sqllogictest -- test/sqllogictest/FILE.slt -v&#96; |[m
[32m+[m[32m| Sources/sinks, Kafka, catalog, pgwire | &#96;test/testdrive/&#96; | &#96;.td&#96; | &#96;cd test/testdrive &amp;&amp; ./mzcompose run default FILE.td&#96; |[m
[32m+[m[32m| Raw pgwire messages (COPY, extended protocol) | &#96;test/pgtest/&#96; | &#96;.pt&#96; | &#96;cargo test -p environmentd pgtest&#96; |[m
[32m+[m[32m| Pure logic, decoding, pure functions | next to code | Rust &#96;#[test]&#96; | &#96;cargo test -p CRATE_NAME&#96; |[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Environment setup[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mBefore running any &#96;cargo&#96; or &#96;bin/&#96; commands, always prefix with:[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[32m+[m[32msource "$HOME/.cargo/env" &amp;&amp;[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m[32mThis ensures the Rust toolchain is available in Claude Code's shell environment.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Steps[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m1. Parse arguments from: $ARGUMENTS[m
[32m+[m[32m   - First arg (optional): test type — &#96;slt&#96;, &#96;td&#96;, &#96;pgtest&#96;, &#96;cargo&#96;, or &#96;add&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m   - Remaining args: file path, pattern, or test name.[m
[32m+[m[32m   - If no args, ask what to test.[m
[32m+[m[32m2. Find the Materialize repo root — check current directory first, and traverse up the directory path until see &#96;src&#96;, &#96;bin&#96;, &#96;ci&#96;, &#96;doc&#96;,[m
[32m+[m[32m3. Based on test type, run the tests[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Running &#96;slt&#96; (sqllogictest)[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[32m+[m[32m./bin/sqllogictest [--release] -- test/sqllogictest/FILE.slt -v[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m[32m- Use &#96;--rewrite-results&#96; if I ask to update expected output.[m
[32m+[m[32m- If a pattern is given instead of a file, search &#96;test/sqllogictest/&#96; for matching files.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Running &#96;td&#96; (testdrive)[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[32m+[m[32mcd test/testdrive[m
[32m+[m[32m./mzcompose run default FILE.td[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Running &#96;pgtest&#96;[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[32m+[m[32mcargo test -p environmentd pgtest[m
[32m+[m[32m# or specific: cargo test -p environmentd test_pgtest_FILE_NAME[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Running &#96;cargo&#96; (Rust unit/integration tests)[m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[32m+[m[32mcargo test -p CRATE_NAME [-- test_name] [--nocapture][m
[32m+[m[32m&#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m### Adding a test (&#96;add&#96;)[m
[32m+[m[32m- Ask what behavior to test.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Determine the right framework based on the table above.[m
[32m+[m[32m- For &#96;.slt&#96;: use &#96;mode cockroach&#96;, test NULLs and edge cases.[m
[32m+[m[32m- For &#96;.pt&#96;: wire new files in &#96;src/environmentd/tests/pgwire.rs&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m- For Rust: use &#96;#[mz_ore::test]&#96; (or &#96;#[mz_ore::test(tokio::test)]&#96; for async).[m
[32m+[m[32m- Do NOT modify files in &#96;test/sqllogictest/sqlite&#96; or &#96;test/sqllogictest/cockroach&#96; (upstream).[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m4. Show full output. If tests fail, show failures clearly and offer to help fix.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mIf more guidance is needed on how to run the test, or set up the environment read &#96;doc/developer/guide-testing.md&#96; and &#96;doc/developer/guide.md&#96;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m## Rules[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m- Always show the full error output before attempting fixes.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Ask before modifying test files.[m
[32m+[m[32m- Do NOT commit test changes — I'll decide when to commit.[m
\ No newline at end of file[m

```
</details>
</tool-use>

# Code review: `local-dev-main...HEAD`

**Base:** `local-dev-main`  
**Files changed:** 1 — `.claude/skills/mz-tests/SKILL.md` (new, +76 lines)

---

## Blocking

**None.** No production code, tests, or config that could break the build or product.

---

## Strong suggestions

1. **Missing newline at end of file**  
   The diff shows `\ No newline at end of file`. Add a final newline so the file is POSIX‑text and plays nicely with tools and git.

2. **Incomplete sentence in Steps**  
   Step 2 ends mid-sentence: “traverse up the directory path until see `src`, `bin`, `ci`, `doc`,”  
   Fix to something like: “…until you see `src`, `bin`, `ci`, or `doc`” (and optionally “then use that as the repo root”).

---

## Nits

- **Consistency with other skills:** If other `.claude/skills` use a trailing newline and a specific style for “Steps” (e.g. “Find repo root: …”), consider aligning this skill with that.
- **pgtest path:** You mention “wire new files in `src/environmentd/tests/pgwire.rs`”. If that path is not the single place to wire pgtest files, a short note like “(or the appropriate test harness)” could avoid over-promising. Only worth changing if the repo layout is more varied.

---

## Summary

Single, focused change: a new skill for running and adding Materialize tests. Content matches the repo’s testing docs (slt/td/pgtest/cargo, `bin/` and `mzcompose` usage). Checklist items for Rust, SQL, errors, architecture, and release notes don’t apply. Address the two strong suggestions (newline + complete Step 2) and you’re in good shape.
